data.title: Sangamo stock rallies 37% on FDA update for Fabry disease drug
data.description:
• Sangamo Therapeutics (NASDAQ:SGMO) shares rallied 37% in early trading Tuesday on news the company can file for FDA accelerated approval of its drug ST-920 for the treatment of Fabry disease without running an additional registrational study.
  • Sangamo said the FDA has agreed the company can use data from its ongoing Phase 1/2 STAAR study as the primary basis for an application under the agency’s accelerated approval program, which should put the company about three years ahead of schedule.
  • The dataset is expected to be available in the first half of 2025, which would enable the company to file for approval in the second half of the year, according to a statement.
  • The company added that it was “continuing to advance ongoing business development discussions with potential collaboration partners.”

data.title: Lode Gold signs agreement to execute spin-out, creating two pure play companies
data.description:
• Lode Gold Resources (TSXV:LOD:CA) announced on Tuesday, its subsidiary, Gold Oregon, has entered into a binding agreement to acquire Great Republic Mining, pursuant to which the GRM and the company's subsidiary propose to complete a Reverse Take Over transaction pursuant to which GRM will acquire all the issued and outstanding shares of the company's subsidiary.
  • The company said, it will be a tax efficient spin out: shareholders of Lode Gold will receive shares of Gold Orogen.
  • The new company, Gold Orogen will have assets in two areas in Canada: Yukon and Atlantic Canada. It will be a focused exploration pure play company.
  • It will launch with funding of over $3 million dollars, with a plan to raise an additional $1.5 million.

data.title: Global Payments lowered to Market Perform at Bernstein on lack of catalysts
data.description:
Bernstein analyst Harshita Rawat downgraded Global Payments (NYSE:GPN) stock to Market Perform from Outperform in a recent note as "we struggle to see upside catalysts over the next few months."

"Although the reset 2025 guidance already partially incorporates this, we also worry about churn dynamics as the company unifies its retail/restaurant POS brands under Genius," Rawat wrote in a note to clients.

The next potentially positive catalysts likely will emerge in mid-2025, she added, as share buybacks are set to commence, "and we are able to see momentum from the turnaround plan."

The downgrade comes despite Global Payments' (GPN) "undemanding" valuation, with the stock down some 22% this year. Seeking Alpha's Quant system gives GPN a valuation grade of "D+," with the poorest mark in price-to-earnings ratio.

Bernstein's Market Perform rate aligns with the Quant rating of Hold and diverges from the average Wall Street analyst rating of Buy.

Global Payments (GPN) is slated to report its Q3 results next week on Oct. 30.

data.title: Peloton stock rises on Costco partnership
data.description:
Peloton Interactive (NASDAQ:PTON) shares popped 2% Tuesday morning after the exercise equipment company announced a partnership with Costco (NASDAQ:COST).

Under the terms of the deal, Costco (COST) will sell Peloton's Bike+ in 300 of its U.S. stores and on Costco.com this holiday season. Starting November 1, Costco members will be able to purchase an exclusive Bike+ bundle through February 15, 2025, or while supplies last.

The Peloton Bike+ is expected to retail for $1,999 in-store and $2,199 online (including delivery), and comes with a 48-month extended warranty.

This partnership marks Peloton’s (PTON) first seasonal retail collaboration in the U.S., enabling the company to gain additional exposure for its premium connected fitness experience among an affluent, net-new audience during the important holiday season.

“The Costco brand is incredibly powerful and respected in the US and abroad. Costco members know they are shopping for high quality products from trusted brands with the best value,” said Dion Camp Sanders, Chief Emerging Business Officer, Peloton. “We’re excited to learn from this first seasonal collaboration in the US and hopefully expand distribution of Peloton products to new geographic markets with Costco in the future.”

data.title: AmpliTech enters 5-year supplier agreement with Fujitsu
data.description:
• AmpliTech Group (NASDAQ:AMPG) signed a 5-year supplier agreement to provide Fujitsu with a varied array of Private 5G Network products, including AmpliTech’s 5G ORAN radios, 5G Customer Premises Equipment, 5G CU/DU and Core Servers as well as other ancillary products required to set a Private 5G Network at the end user’s site.
  • The agreement involves supplying Fujitsu with a complete suite of Private 5G Network products tailored to meet the specific needs of their end customers, ensuring enhanced connectivity, while also providing signal reliability and security.
  • This agreement covers Europe, Africa and the Middle East.

data.title: Indaptus Therapeutics signs clinical supply agreement with Beigene
data.description:
• Indaptus Therapeutics (NASDAQ:INDP) announced a clinical supply agreement with BeiGene (NASDAQ:BGNE) .
  • Indaptus plans to advance human clinical evaluation of the combination of BeiGene's anti-PD-1 antibody, tislelizumab, with Indaptus’ Decoy20, a novel treatment designed to induce a broad immune response to fight cancer.
  • Indaptus will seek approval from the FDA to initiate a combination trial, which is anticipated to begin in 2025.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: Capital City Bank GAAP EPS of $0.78 beats by $0.01, revenue of $59.72M beats by $1.02M
data.description:
• Capital City Bank press release (NASDAQ:CCBG): Q3 GAAP EPS of $0.78 beats by $0.01.
  • Revenue of $59.72M (+6.6% Y/Y) beats by $1.02M.

data.title: ASML CEO says US plans to continue applying export pressure to China: report
data.description:
ASML Holding (NASDAQ:ASML) CEO Christophe Fouquet said the U.S. will continue to pursue restricting certain high-tech exports to China, and pressure allies to do the same, regardless of who wins the presidential election next month, according to a report by Reuters.

"If you look at the geopolitical landscape, I think it's clear that the United States will continue to apply pressure on their side for more restriction," Fouquet said.

Fouquet made the comments during a technology conference in London on Tuesday.

The Netherlands-based semiconductor equipment manufacturer still expects its sales to grow in 2025, although at a slower pace, and 2026.

ASML shares plunged on October 15 following its latest financial report, which revealed export sales to China would decline to 20% of total revenue in 2025 after accounting for 49% during Q2 2024. The projected decline is due in part to the Netherlands enacting stricter export controls for advanced semiconductor manufacturing equipment in September.

Fouquet said the Netherlands and Europe are debating the future of further restrictions, according to Reuters.

ASML's largest single customer continues to be Taiwan Semiconductor Manufacturing (TSM). The U.S. Commerce Department is apparently investigating if TSM, which builds Nvidia's (NVDA) highly demanded AI chips, is also building smartphone or AI chips for China's Huawei.

ASML shares have steadily declined since mid-July, when they were closing at more than $1,000. They closed down another 1% on Monday to settle at $714.10.

data.title: Barksdale announces shares for debt transaction
data.description:
• Barksdale Resources (OTCQX:BRKCF) on Tuesday said it has entered into a debt settlement agreement to settle an aggregate of C$41,367 in bona fide debt owing to an arm's-length creditor of the company by issuing 258,543 common shares at a deemed price of C$0.16 per debt settlement share.
  • Barksdale said it intends to satisfy the indebtedness with the debt settlement shares in order to preserve its cash for development of its business.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: Scorpius Holdings announces partnership with U.S. biotech company
data.description:
• Scorpius Holdings (NYSE:SCPX) has announced a strategic partnership with a U.S.-based biotech company.
  • Under this agreement, the biotech company will work with Scorpius' program management team, with plans to transfer a research cell bank to Scorpius’ state-of-the-art facilities in San Antonio, TX for future biomanufacturing activities.
  • With this partnership, the Co. continue to fulfill the critical need for secure, U.S.-based development and manufacturing support for groundbreaking biologics research.
  • “This partnership underscores Scorpius' ability to support biotech companies at every stage of their journey, from innovative research to clinical trials,” said Jeff Wolf, CEO of Scorpius.
